=====On hand=====
{{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}}
This listing is of Volkner's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Volkner
|pkmn=Raichu
|gender=male
|stage1=Pikachu
|type1=Electric
|img=Volkner Pikachu.png
|cap=Volkner's Pikachu
|img2=Volkner Raichu.png
|cap2=Volkner's Raichu
|epnum=DP165
|epname=Flint Sparks the Fire!
|vajp=Satsuki Yukino
|vajpnote=as Pikachu
|vajp2=Chie Satō
|vajp2note=as Raichu
|vaen=Satsuki Yukino
|vaennote=as Pikachu
|vaen2=Chie Satō
|vaen2note=as Raichu
|desc={{p|Raichu}} is Volkner's very first Pokémon. He first appeared in ''[[DP165|Flint Sparks the Fire!]]'', where he reunited with [[Flint]], and in the [[DP166|next episode]]. Raichu has been shown to be {{pkmn2|Walking|outside of his Poké Ball}} most of the time.

Volkner first received his Raichu as a {{p|Pikachu}} when he was young. He battled with [[Flint's Chimchar]] and lost. However, in their [[rematch]], Pikachu defeated {{p|Chimchar}} with {{m|Iron Tail}}. Since then, Flint and Volkner became friends and rivals. Later, Pikachu was battling the [[Proprietor]]'s {{p|Houndoom}}, when the latter was a [[Pokémon hunter|Pokémon poacher]] at the time. Houndoom dealt some damage to Chimchar and Pikachu, but Chimchar wasn't able to continue, leaving Pikachu to do it on its own. Volkner was very determined to battle the Proprietor. After a long battle, the Proprietor was defeated and became friends with Volkner and Flint.

Pikachu later [[Evolution|evolved]] into a Raichu prior to Volkner becoming the [[Sunyshore City]] [[Gym Leader]]. Raichu was very strong as he defeated several Pokémon such as a {{pkmn|Trainer}}'s {{p|Weavile}} and {{p|Mothim}} over time. However, Volkner did not select Raichu in any of his matches against {{Ash}}.

Raichu briefly appeared alongside Volkner during the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''.

Raichu's known moves are {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Thunderbolt}}, {{m|Volt Tackle}}, and {{m|Thunder}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Volkner
|pkmn=Luxray
|type1=Electric
|gender=male
|img=Volkner Luxray.png
|epnum=DP166
|epname=The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore!
|vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi
|vajpnote=[[DP166]]-[[DP179]]
|vajp2=Kensuke Satō
|vajp2note=[[JN077]]
|vaen=Sarah Natochenny
|desc=Volkner's {{p|Luxray}} appeared for the first time in ''[[DP166|The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore!]]'', where he {{pkmn|battle}}d [[Ash's Grotle]] during {{Ash}}'s [[Gym]] challenge. However, the battle was called off due to {{TRT}} terrorizing the city.

During the [[rematch]], Luxray first battled with {{AP|Pikachu}}, and won after taking hits from several {{m|Iron Tail}}s. Ash next used {{AP|Infernape}}, which got badly injured during the battle. Due to this, his [[Ability]] {{a|Blaze}} got activated. However, this time {{p|Infernape}} was able to control his Ability, and with the powers gained through it, the Flame Pokémon quickly defeated Luxray, serving the final blow with a devastating {{m|Flame Wheel}}, earning Ash the {{Badge|Beacon}}. Luxray was seen in a flashback in ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]''.

Luxray reappeared in ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'', where he was the first Pokémon in Volkner's [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. Luxray first went up against {{AP|Lucario}} and {{AP|Gengar}} before being recalled. He later battled Lucario once more, with the battle ending in a tie.

Luxray's known moves are {{m|Spark}}, {{m|Thunder}}, {{m|Shock Wave}}, {{m|Thunder Fang}}, {{m|Double Team}}, {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Electric Terrain}}, {{m|Rising Voltage}}, and {{m|Snarl}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Volkner
|pkmn=Electivire
|type1=Electric
|img=Volkner Electivire.png
|epnum=DP179
|epname=The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World!
|vajp=Yūichi Nakamura
|vajpnote=[[JN077]]
|vaen=Bill Rogers
|desc={{p|Electivire}} was the first Pokémon which Volkner used during his [[rematch]] against {{Ash}}. Ash first used {{AP|Torterra}}, but it was easily defeated. Next Ash chose to use {{p|Pikachu}}. With his {{m|Thunderbolt}}, Pikachu triggered Electivire's [[Ability]], making it an even harder target to hit. However, when one of Electivire's punches connected with Pikachu, the Thunderbolt Pokémon was {{status|Paralysis|paralyzed}} by Pikachu's {{a|Static}}. After this, Pikachu was able to finish Electivire with a well-aimed {{m|Iron Tail}}.

Electivire reappeared in ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'', when it was the third Pokémon in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. It was used to negate Pikachu's Thunderbolt with its Motor Drive Ability. After Pikachu, it briefly battled Ash's Lucario before being recalled. Electivire later battled Ash's Pikachu again, but it was eventually defeated by Pikachu's {{m|10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt}}, which proved to be too much for Motor Drive to absorb.

In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Electivire was shown watching {{an|Cynthia}}'s [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Iris}} alongside its Trainer.

Electivire's known moves are {{m|Fire Punch}}, {{m|Ice Punch}}, {{m|Iron Tail}}, {{m|Wild Charge}}, and {{m|Thunder Punch}}, and its Ability is {{a|Motor Drive}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Volkner
|pkmn=Jolteon
|type1=Electric
|img=Volkner Jolteon.png
|epnum=DP179
|epname=The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World!
|vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima
|vaen=Kayzie Rogers
|desc={{p|Jolteon}} was the second Pokémon used by Volkner during his and {{Ash}}'s [[rematch]]. It has impressive agility and was shown to be able to use consecutive Shadow Ball attacks. Ash chose to use his {{AP|Infernape}}, which soon defeated the Lightning Pokémon with a {{m|Mach Punch}}.

Jolteon's known moves are {{m|Thunderbolt}} and {{m|Shadow Ball}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Volkner
|pkmn=Rotom
|type1=Electric
|type2=Flying
|img=Volkner Fan Rotom.png
|epnum=JN077
|epname=Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!
|vajp=
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Rotom}} was the second Pokémon used by Volkner in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against Ash. After using {{m|Thunder Wave}} on {{AP|Gengar}}, Rotom was able to defeat it with {{m|Hex}}. Rotom was recalled when {{AP|Pikachu}} trapped it with {{m|Electroweb}} and was about to use {{m|Thunderbolt}} on it. Later, Rotom battled against Lucario, weakening it with Hex after it was paralyzed, only to be defeated by a {{m|Reversal}}.

Rotom's known moves are {{m|Air Slash}}, {{m|Thunder Wave}}, and {{m|Hex}}.}}
